text: The surgeon gradually enlarges the retroperitoneal space by removing fat tissue outside Gerota's fascia, revealing clear anatomical landmarks. The space expands significantly as the fat is pushed away, allowing for better visualization of the surgical field. The surgeon identifies the upper landmark of the genitofemoral nerve and opens the fascia in the middle of the psoas muscle.

The surgeon identifies the plane outside the anterior surface of the kidney, preparing to approach the crossing vessel. The vessel is visible, and care is taken to avoid cutting into it during the dissection.

The surgeon isolates the artery within the Gerota's fascia, opening the surrounding fat tissue to access the renal sinus space. The camera view reveals the tumor location and the single artery, which appears relatively small.
